title: Reject linear stacking without visual hierarchy
summary: Models default to stacking every section vertically with identical spacing and visual weight — hero, features, testimonials, pricing, CTA — producing a 'scroll wallpaper' where no section is visually prioritized and the page reads as a flat sequence.
instruction: Do not stack full-width sections in identical style without establishing a clear visual hierarchy among them. At least one section must be visually dominant (different background, larger padding, distinct color treatment, or non-standard layout). If every section has the same background, same padding, same width, then none guides the eye.
rationale: A page where every section has identical visual weight forces the user to scan linearly with no entry point. Visual hierarchy in section treatments helps users orient: the hero should feel like an entry, the CTA like a destination, and supporting sections like middle content they can skip or absorb.
violations: A landing page with six sections, each with white background, 80px padding, centered layout, and no background image or color break — the page is a uniform white column.
A SaaS site where testimonials use the same background and padding as the feature grid and the hero — nothing signals which content is primary.
A blog article page where the hero, body, author bio, comments, and related posts all share the same background and internal padding — no section boundary cues.
controls: A landing page with a dark hero (full-viewport height), light feature grid (alternating backgrounds), dark testimonials (image background), and highlighted CTA (brand color strip).
A product page with a full-bleed hero, contained feature section with alternating row backgrounds, and an immersive demo section with a dark backdrop that breaks the pattern.
A documentation site using a consistent content-container width but varying the background treatment for section types: white for docs, light gray for related resources, dark for code-only sections.
borderlineCases: A single-purpose landing page (one message, one CTA) that intentionally uses identical visual weight across its two sections because there is no secondary content.
contextualExceptions: Long-form editorial content where uniform reading experience is the goal and section hierarchy is expressed exclusively through headings, not layout.
Application interfaces where tool windows are deliberately uniform to avoid distracting from the content.
detectorSignal: Three or more consecutive full-width sections sharing the same background-color, padding, and layout mode with no alternating visual treatment
matcherProposal: Detect major layout sections (section, article, [class*=section], [class*=panel]) that are full-width (>= 90% viewport). Group them sequentially. If three or more consecutive sections share the same background-color, padding-top, and layout column count, flag for review.
detectorLimitations: Cannot detect visual hierarchy expressed through imagery, typography size, or imagery that differs while structural CSS is identical.
Hero sections with background images that share the same CSS class as text-only sections will not flag despite different visual weight.
CSS custom properties that change background between sections at the cascade level may not be detected as different.
falsePositiveScenarios: A brand that deliberately uses a consistent visual container, relying entirely on content imagery and headings to establish section hierarchy — the CSS is identical but the visual result is distinct.
A design system's section components that wrap different background colors via inline styles or utility classes not captured by the heuristic.
mitigationStrategy: Lower to warning. Only flag when consecutive sections exceed 4 and no section contains a non-white background or distinct layout grid. Use CSS custom-property resolution where possible.

fixturePlans.violation: A landing page with six sections all using .section { background: #fff; padding: 80px 24px; max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; } — uniform white blocks from hero to footer.
fixturePlans.control: A landing page with alternating dark hero image section, white feature grid, gray testimonial section (#F9FAFB), and brand-colored CTA strip (#1D4ED8).
fixturePlans.borderline: A single-message landing page with two sections (value prop + CTA) both using the same white background — acceptable because there is no secondary content.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
overlapNotes: Related to layout.centered-everything but focuses on section-level stacking without hierarchy rather than per-element centering. No overlap with existing rules.
